question.text: Advisory fees on a segregated portfolio are:

answer.text: Charged at a reduced rate of 50% of the main portfolio TER
Charged only when recovery exceeds 90% of the original value
Not permitted to be charged on the segregated portfolio
Charged at the same rate as the main portfolio

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI prohibits charging advisory fees on the segregated portfolio. The TER on the segregated portfolio is limited to expenses (excluding advisory) on a pro-rata recovery basis only. This protects investors from paying for a failed credit call. **The Trap:** Some expenses (custodian, RTA, audit) can be charged pro-rata on recovery, but advisory/management fees are completely prohibited on the segregated portion. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is a key investor protection measure in the side pocketing framework. It holds the fund manager accountable for the credit call that led to the segregation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The no-advisory-fee rule is a strong investor protection. Explain to clients that they won't be paying the fund manager for holding a defaulted asset. They pay fees only on the performing portfolio where active management continues.
